title: Request Catalog Correlation — compact analytical snapshot
preview:
Purpose and scope:

This is a compact working snapshot that maps recent customer asks (Q4 2029–Q1 2030) into correlated request clusters, quick-win opportunities, and tech/product risk flags. The goal is not to be exhaustive — instead, produce a tight, actionable catalog that partner teams (product, eng, optimize) c...
text:
Purpose and scope:

This is a compact working snapshot that maps recent customer asks (Q4 2029–Q1 2030) into correlated request clusters, quick-win opportunities, and tech/product risk flags. The goal is not to be exhaustive — instead, produce a tight, actionable catalog that partner teams (product, eng, optimize) can scan and act on in 30–60 minutes.

Method:
- Source inputs: support tickets, onboarding notes, CSM call transcripts, trial account audits, and two field workshops (EMEA, NA).
- Auto-clustered by semantic grouping then manually reconciled to avoid false merges (common failure: conflating \"model selection\" asks with \"routing / fallback\" asks).
- Each cluster gets: representative microstory, frequency band (low/med/high), estimated impact (qualitative), 1–2 proposed fixes (owner + size), and immediate mitigation if required.

Top-level findings (high level):
- A small n...

title: Customer briefing: ingress queue latency surge — recovery options & verification
preview: TL;DR: On 2029-10-02 18:17 UTC a sustained latency surge impacted the ingress queue in our eu-west region for ~2 hours, causing delayed responses and increased timeouts for some customer requests. We implemented a graduated mitigation (priority drain + temporary rate-limits + regional redeploy) which restored normal...
text:
TL;DR: On 2029-10-02 18:17 UTC a sustained latency surge impacted the ingress queue in our eu-west region for ~2 hours, causing delayed responses and increased timeouts for some customer requests. We implemented a graduated mitigation (priority drain + temporary rate-limits + regional redeploy) which restored normal throughput by 20:45 UTC. This doc outlines the customer-facing statuspage blurb, a succinct post-incident summary you can share with stakeholders, verification steps customers can run, reconciliation/replay options, and our next steps including compensation and technical remediation.

Statuspage draft (short):
- Incident start: 2029-10-02 18:17 UTC
- Impact: Elevated request latency and intermittent timeouts for API calls routed through eu-west ingress. Affected tenants saw delayed responses and occasional 5xx errors.
- Status: Service restored (partial recovery at 19:50 U...

text:
Purpose: informal working notes for Western Hemisphere surge anticipation. Use this as a single source-of-truth for the next 7-day prewarming/backfill plan, quick experiments, and open questions for the infra sync. This is intentionally rough.

Context:
- Recent traffic spikes from a major customer in NA and a marketing campaign in SA create a likely 2-4x short-term uplift for chat endpoints starting ~2029-07-21 00:00 UTC.
- We need a low-friction hedging plan that preserves latency SLOs for paid customers and keeps unit cost growth bounded.
- This doc ties into the global surge playbook but focuses on Western Hemisphere specifics (latency corridors, cloud regions, and cross-region backfill).

Quick summary (TL;DR):
- Recommended prewarm schedule: +60 GPUs in us-central1 and +30 GPUs in us-west1 on 2029-07-20 03:00 UTC (24h prewarm window), and +20 GPUs in sa-east1 on 2029-07-20 05:00...

title: conversation-latency-thrift-benchmark-lattice
preview: TL;DR: quick hands-on benchmark comparing three serving variants (base-fp16, q8-fast, q4-hybrid) across conversation lengths and burst patterns. Goal: find low-cost operating points that keep p95 latency under 300ms for short-turn chat (<=512 tokens) while identifying where KV-cache and prefetching buy the most. Thi...
text:
TL;DR: quick hands-on benchmark comparing three serving variants (base-fp16, q8-fast, q4-hybrid) across conversation lengths and burst patterns. Goal: find low-cost operating points that keep p95 latency under 300ms for short-turn chat (<=512 tokens) while identifying where KV-cache and prefetching buy the most. This is a working notebook-style writeup — experimental notes + ascii charts + actionable hypotheses.

Test matrix and setup:
- Workload: conversational chat mimic (user prompt 30–60 tokens, assistant gen 20–300 tokens depending on scenario). Synthetic arrival patterns: steady (10 qps), bursty (20s bursts at 80 qps every 5 minutes), microbursts (5s at 250 qps).
- Models/variants tested:
 * base-fp16: full-precision serving (reference)
 * q8-fast: 8-bit quant with reduced kernel precision, optimized for throughput
 * q4-hybrid: aggressive 4-bit quant with mixed kernels (prefetc...

Purpose:
This doc is a concise, customer-oriented explanation of a recent scaled-throttle event that affected a subset of heavy-throughput customers. It contains: a short public-friendly status snippet for the status page, recommended actions customers can take immediately, a customer-facing postmortem summary suitable for email/reporting, and a short timeline + next steps we will take. Use these verbatim or adapt to account teams' tone.

Executive summary (one paragraph):
On 2029-03-17 at ~21:40 UTC we observed sustained request bursts from several integrations which triggered an automated scaled-throttling policy. Customers experienced elevated 429/503 errors and increased tail latency for requests sent during the burst window (~21:40–22:15 UTC). The runtime applied protective throttles to preserve cluster availability; this prevented broader outage but caused degraded experience fo...

title: Precision fleet supply blend optimizer
preview:
Executive summary:
This worksheet explores an optimized fleet composition (the "blend") across precision tiers and batching strategies to meet Q1 2029 regional SLAs while minimizing $/generated-token and bounding observable quality regressions on our eval suites. The goal is a pragmatic mix of low-bit (4-bit/INT4), ...
text:
Executive summary:
This worksheet explores an optimized fleet composition (the "blend") across precision tiers and batching strategies to meet Q1 2029 regional SLAs while minimizing $/generated-token and bounding observable quality regressions on our eval suites. The goal is a pragmatic mix of low-bit (4-bit/INT4), mid-bit (8-bit/INT8), and high-precision nodes to serve our typical production mix: 60% short-chat, 30% assistant-longform, 10% embedding/reprompt workloads.

Scope and objective:
- Time horizon: January–March 2029 capacity planning horizon (prep for sales commitments and Dedicated reservations).
- Regions: NA, EU, APAC (priority APAC capacity lift).
- Deliverable: suggested percent allocation per region to each precision tier + recommended batching target ranges and KV cache settings.

Key assumptions (baseline workload):
- 80M-1B token/month aggregate projected growth + s...

title: Contention ledger: latency & dollar snapshot
preview:
Purpose:

Quick internal snapshot: small lab that tries to apportion latency and dollar impact to tenant-induced contention on our multiplexing slot allocator. Goal is a compact artifact teams can reference when designing routing/policies and when we discuss multitenant pricing tradeoffs. Still rough (notes + charts...
text:
Purpose:

Quick internal snapshot: small lab that tries to apportion latency and dollar impact to tenant-induced contention on our multiplexing slot allocator. Goal is a compact artifact teams can reference when designing routing/policies and when we discuss multitenant pricing tradeoffs. Still rough (notes + charts in ascii).

High-level findings (short):
- Under mixed sessions (heterogeneous prompt lengths) contention creates asymmetric p99 tax: heavy-key tenants produce >2.2x p99 uplift for light-tenants during overlapping windows.
- Observable unit-dollar delta per-100k tokens served increases nonlinearly with contention; first 20% slot saturation is cheap to absorb, after that marginal $/k tokens rises quickly.
- Prefetch/early-accept heuristics reduce p50 and p90 for light-tenants but barely move p99 without admission control.

Experiment setup (lab):
- Runtime: dev-gpu-pool-3 (...

text:
Purpose: quick synthesis of recurring customer asks around per-request stamping/provenance + a short prioritized playlist of small fixes and probes we can ship in 1–3 sprints.

Context snapshot (why this doc):
- Over the last 6 weeks, CS triage has flagged ~28 asks that map to a common theme: customers want more deterministic, lightweight metadata attached to responses (trace IDs, model-version stamps, cost-estimates) and API flags to control whether that metadata is present, and at what granularity.
- These asks appear across usage patterns: high-volume embedding pipelines, multi-tenant inference (Dedicated customers), and Private deployments where auditability is required.
- Customers use terms interchangeably: "response provenance", "token-level stamp", "request trace", "model-fingerprint", "response-cost-preview". Need canonical defs before engineering work.

Raw clustered signals...

text:
Weekly capture for the compact-quant sprint — informal field notes and experiment rollups. Intended for the Applied ML squad and infra partners. No formal conclusions yet; focus is on what crashed, what looked promising, and the sharp edges we need to protect prod rollouts with.

Attendees: Ava (notes), Liam, Priya, Omar, Zoe, SRE: Ben.

Goals this week
- Triage unexpected perplexity regressions observed in int4 compact builds for atlas-13b-compact.
- Validate KV-cache compatibility with compact-quant path (4-bit packing) under bursty interactive traffic.
- Produce a short onboarding checklist + gating evals for new compact variants.

Quick summary / headline findings
- Atlas-13b-compact (int4+layerwise block quant) passes latency SLO in dedicated behind-capacity tests: median RT ~72ms on p4d-smoke nodes for 1-token streaming. Tail (p95) increased to ~220ms when KV-cache miss rate > 3...

text:
Draft customer-facing summary and guidance (DO NOT SEND until legal & eng sign-off)

Executive summary (one sentence)
Between 2028-03-21T15:07:00Z and 2028-03-21T18:21:00Z a subset of requests that requested structured/JSON outputs experienced truncation at the token boundary during streaming responses, which resulted in malformed or incomplete payloads for some customers using both hosted API and Dedicated instances. We identified the root cause and implemented mitigations; a permanent fix is scheduled for automatic rollout and customers with affected data will be offered assistance to reconcile impacted outputs.

Who is impacted
- Customers using structured output / function-calling or JSON templates where downstream systems parse the model output as strict JSON.
- Primarily observed in US-WEST-2 region and in Dedicated pools with burst batching enabled.
- Estimated impact: ~0.8% of...

title: Relayband latency vs $/QPS quickscan
preview:
Purpose:
This is a short exploratory quickscan of "relayband" scheduling (local term for a hybrid short-slot + background-tiling scheduler) to see whether we can get a Pareto improvement for microburst-heavy conversational workloads: improve p99 without increasing per-QPS dollar burn materially. Not a full experimen...
text:
Purpose:
This is a short exploratory quickscan of "relayband" scheduling (local term for a hybrid short-slot + background-tiling scheduler) to see whether we can get a Pareto improvement for microburst-heavy conversational workloads: improve p99 without increasing per-QPS dollar burn materially. Not a full experiment log — thinking sketch + annotated numbers and immediate next steps.

Background / motivation:
- We frequently see customers with mixed workloads: many short chat turns (10-60 tokens) interleaved with occasional long generations.
- Conventional tiling / slot approaches either favor throughput (tiling) or latency (dedicated ephemeral slots). At scale, neither dominates: tiling saves $/token but increases p99 for short-turn bursts.
- Relayband idea: keep a small fraction of fast short-lived slots (relay lanes) that handle sub-100-token turns and fall back to tiling when occu...

text:
Purpose
-----
Quick internal playbook for synthesizing customer feedback (micro-asks) into action lanes that product + eng can execute quickly. Focus is on: velocity (ship small wins), clarity (what problem is solved), and economics (cost/latency impact for inference). This is intentionally lightweight — meant for CS reps during weekly triage + product scoping sessions.

Why this exists
--------------
- We see a high volume of short, tactical asks (e.g., per-route timeout tweak, model fallback config, improved error messages for rate-limits).
- Those asks often sit in queues or get lumped into larger epics and never shipped.
- Small fixes can materially reduce churn and unlock enterprise deals if surfaced with risk/cost impact.

Heuristic scoring (quick 5-point rubric)
-----------------------------------
Use this at the top of every ticket/signal we surface. Score each 1–5, then sum. ...

title: Inference demand blueprint - tokens & QPS forecast (2027 Q1)
preview:
Summary:
This is a working planning sheet for projected request demand (tokens/sec and QPS) across regions for the Redwood Hosted API for 2027 Q1. Purpose: produce conservative GPU fleet targets and headroom policies that satisfy 99th percentile latency SLOs for the majority of end-customer workloads while minimizin...
text:
Summary:
This is a working planning sheet for projected request demand (tokens/sec and QPS) across regions for the Redwood Hosted API for 2027 Q1. Purpose: produce conservative GPU fleet targets and headroom policies that satisfy 99th percentile latency SLOs for the majority of end-customer workloads while minimizing overprovisioning.

Key conclusions (top-line):
- Baseline expected 2027Q1 peak (business-as-usual) = 1.85M tokens/sec globally (aggregate at 95th percentile daily peaks).
- Conservative planning target (headroom margin) = baseline * 1.5 => 2.78M tokens/sec to reserve across fleet for predictable latency.
- Operational emergency capacity (fallback / best-effort) target = 1.0x additional (total theoretical capacity target ~= 5.56M tokens/sec when including emergency fallback variants).
- Regional allocation: US-West 38%, US-East 26%, EU-West 22%, APAC-SG 14% (alloc by obser...

preview:

The aim: compute tokens/sec and QPS headroom required to absorb 15-minute sustained multipliers, map those to GPU slices ...
text:
Purpose:
This working-sheet captures a focused set of 15-minute sustained burst scenarios ("tailwind" bursts) that historically have caused the largest transient stress on the Hosted API ingress. The aim: compute tokens/sec and QPS headroom required to absorb 15-minute sustained multipliers, map those to GPU slices across regions, and produce a recommended safety-stock (warm prewarmed GPUs + spare rotational slices) target for Q1 2027 planning.

Context & motivation:
- Most existing capacity docs focus on hourly/day peaks. However, product launches, marketing-induced campaigns, and a handful of viral events create 10–30 minute sustained bursts that require different heuristics (prewarm time, cold-start fraction, KV cache warmup).
- We want a short-run safety stock that minimizes tail-latency SLO breaches while keeping monthly committed GPU costs reasonable.
- This file experiments wit...

Purpose / TL;DR:
This is a working synthesis of customer feedback threads from Jul-Sep 2026 focused on request provenance (where asks come from), repeat ask patterns, and lightweight fix proposals. Goal: give PM + Eng a short list of low-effort, high-confidence patches and a provenance map so we can triage ownership. Not a final product — notes + unresolved questions inline.

How I pulled this together:
- Sources: Support tickets (top 150 by frequency), NPS followups, 1:1s, renewal calls, pilot telemetry, and 3 Slack threads (see linked_artifacts).
- Method: Rapid clustering (manual), then trace each cluster to origin channel and last-touch (who responded). Marked whether ask is: UX friction, API/behavior mismatch, observability gap, performance/cost, or security/compliance.
- Confidence: high for frequency and clear asks; medium where requests are ambiguous; low where we only have si...

Mul...
text:
Purpose:
Quick synthesis of recent field conversations about KV/prefix cache behavior, tail-latency incidents, and a compact prioritized feature request list for eng/observability to triage. This is intentionally light-weight — raw vignettes + proposed low-effort probes first.

Executive summary (one-paragraph):
Multiple customers running chat-heavy pipelines report intermittent tail latency spikes that correlate with cache-miss patterns (large, variable context windows and frequent model-rolls). Customers want: (1) clearer per-customer cache hit metrics surfaced in Console/APIs, (2) a lightweight cache-warming / pinning primitive for critical prefixes, and (3) safer fallback behaviors when cache thrash occurs (e.g., short-term autoscale to avoid dropped requests or automatic model-variant fallback). These ask lanes are high impact for churn-sensitive accounts and low-to-medium implem...

text:
Background:
This is a working runbook for simulated peak events we might see in Q4 2026. Purpose is to produce quick, actionable headroom targets (tokens/sec and QPS) and identify which GPU pools and regions to scale during 30m-2h surges. Not a finalized plan — notes + numbers are intentionally conservative and include assumptions to revisit with metrics team.

Key assumptions (baseline):
- Traffic mix: 70% short-chat (mean 40 tokens/request), 25% assistant/long-form (mean 480 tokens/request), 5% embeddings/rerank (avg 120 tokens/request).
- Avg tokens per request (weighted) = 0.7*40 + 0.25*480 + 0.05*120 = 40*0.7 + 120 + 6 = 28 + 120 + 6 = 154 tokens/request.
- Baseline QPS -> tokens/sec mapping: tokens/sec = QPS * 154.
- Effective tokens/sec per GPU (mixed fleet): see fleet mix below. These are conservative throughput numbers measured from bench runs with batch size 8 and KV cache 2...

Purpose: Rapidly surface friction points in the new quickstart flows and lightweight SDK samples. This sprint day was focused on trying the quickstarts end-to-end on macOS and Linux, and running the JS + Python sample apps with minimal credentials.

Summary highlights:
- Overall: quickstarts run but several gotchas for first-run developers (auth, env vars, package versions). Good baseline but not PLG-ready without small fixes.
- Biggest UX blockers today: opaque auth error messages; sample repo has 2 outdated deps; README example for streaming uses old API shape.
- Positive: containerized quickstart (docker-compose) worked consistently for team members with constrained envs — keep as fallback.

Executive summary:

This note collates 32 customer touchpoints (support threads, partner calls, and in-product telemetry) into a compact set of problem statements and low-effort probe ideas we can land in the next 2–6 weeks. Goal: reduce top-two friction signals feeding into latency/cost complaints and create measurable improvement metrics we can report in the Aug 12 customer forum.

Why \"muon\"?
- Lightweight name for a focused deliverable: fast-moving, penetrating the noise.
- Use when we want short cycles: observe -> small fix -> measure -> iterate.

Context & scope:
- Data sources: support tickets (126–140 range), three enterprise pilot interviews (FinLens, HealthOps, ChatMate), two SRE on-call digests, and token-cost telemetry from hosted-api per-customer rollups.
- Scope: inference-side behavioral asks (not billing/package changes). Primarily kv-cache ergonomics, batching defau...

text:
Purpose:
Provide a compact, slice-level weekly sampler of GPU utilization across a representative subset of clusters and pools. Not a full fleet export — this is intended for quick triage and to highlight latent fragmentation / microburst patterns that the daily totals miss.

Notes on methodology:
- Sampling window: 2026-07-02T00:00 -> 2026-07-08T23:59 (ISO week)
- Sample selection: 18 clusters chosen to cover large/medium/small pools, one cluster per region priority (NA/EU/APAC) and 3 cross-region multi-pool clusters.
- Metric aggregation: per-pool per-cluster rolling 5m avg, median, p95, peak-minute, and microburst_count (>= 80% -> 100% spike within 60s).
- Fragmentation index: heuristic = 1 - (sum(continuous-slot-chunks)/total-available-slots) -> 0..1 where higher means more fragmentation.
- Cold-starts excluded for pools with >50% cold-start rate during window (see notes column).
...

title: Voice Handoff — Gap Analysis and Requests (May 2026)
preview:
TL;DR:
A compact synthesis of recent CS-to-Engineering handoffs around customer runtime problems (latency spikes, stale KV cache behavior, and model-fallback surprise). Customers are asking for: clearer fallbacks, per-client caching controls, deterministic canary controls, and a lightweight diagnostic envelope they ...
text:
TL;DR:
A compact synthesis of recent CS-to-Engineering handoffs around customer runtime problems (latency spikes, stale KV cache behavior, and model-fallback surprise). Customers are asking for: clearer fallbacks, per-client caching controls, deterministic canary controls, and a lightweight diagnostic envelope they can ship to ops. This note consolidates raw threads, prioritizes requests by customer impact and implementation risk, and proposes two small experiments that could buy time while larger platform changes are scoped.

Background / Why this matters:
- Over the last 8 weeks the CS team handled ~18 escalations that surfaced the same root ambiguities: when Redwood falls back to a cheaper/shorter model variant, customers often see inconsistent outputs and don’t know whether fallback occurred.
- Several customers using Dedicated pools also reported inconsistent KV cache hits after ...

Purpose:
This note captures the outcomes of the regional preemption exercise run on 2026-04-21 targeting the EU-west and US-west fleets. The goal was to validate our automated fallback routing and reclaim capacity on preemptible pools without violating latency SLOs for Reserved customers. This is a working log (informal) — please add edits inline.

Attendees:
- Ava Chen (owner) - infra
- Diego Martinez - fleet ops
- Hannah Patel - SRE
- Liam O'Connor - serving-runtime
- Sofia Nguyen - product-dedicated
- Observers: 2 on-call engineers from support

Exercise summary (what we did):
1) Triggered scheduled preemption on a subset of ephemeral nodes in eu-west-2b (approx 160 GPUs) at 14:00 UTC.
2) Simultaneously triggered a low-priority drain on a subset of us-west-1 preemptible cells (approx 120 GPUs) to validate cross-region routing.

text:
Purpose:
This is a working sketch of a small-burst morphology weve been calling the 'cicada' pattern: short, irregular spikes at 50-400ms duration that repeatedly punctuate an otherwise low baseline. The goal is to capture observed latency shape, cost impact by model size and quant profile, and quick ideas for tooling/alerts. Not a polished report — notes, ASCII charts, and quick heuristics for ops/product to chew on.

Experiment summary:
- Date range: 2026-04-06 -> 2026-04-11 (sampled 1s resolution traces)
- Targets: hosted-api fleet (region: us-west-2), vCPU+GPU slot model variants: tiny/compact/standard (approx 3B/7B/13B)
- Conditions: 1) cold-start disabled (warm pool), 2) KV-cache enabled for session prefix where noted, 3) quantized vs fp16 models compared
- Key metrics captured: p50/p90/p95/p99 latency (ms), tokens/sec throughput, cost-per-token (USD per 1k tokens), tail-duratio...

Purpose:

Purpose:
This doc is a lightweight, tactical cookbook for running our Q3-bridge quarter (internal name: "Orbit"). It combines a proposed OKR crosswalk, an all-staff agenda skeleton, operational playbook snippets, and a compact risk/mitigation register for exec review. Intended audience: execs, leads across Eng, Product, GTM, and Ops. Status: working draft — looking for feedback from product-ops, eng-sre, and head of GTM by 2026-03-19.

Executive narrative (1-paragraph):
We are entering a three-month bridge focused on reliability-at-scale while preserving velocity for model onboarding and Dedicated sales. The priority is threefold: (1) harden the serving runtime for predictable tail latency across published models, (2) reduce per-token unit cost on hosted API by 8–12% through batching and caching recommendations, and (3) finalize Private deployment packaging for two pilot customers. Th...

preview:

Focus: clear first-time success, deterministic fallbacks, and lightweight telemetry so we can iterate on onboarding metrics wit...
text:
Purpose:
A compact working doc for the new "quicksplice" playground flow — a tiny, low-friction SDK+playground bundle engineers can use to prototype Redwood API integrations in <10 minutes. Focus: clear first-time success, deterministic fallbacks, and lightweight telemetry so we can iterate on onboarding metrics without heavy instrumentation drain.

Context and motivation:
- We keep seeing friction in first 10-minute runs: env var confusion, token quota surprises, and streaming failures that stop the demo.
- Quicksplice = opinionated minimal runner (JS + Python examples) + sandbox prompts + an auto-fallback policy (hosted -> dedicated-dryrun -> noop simulator) so demos never hard-fail.
- This doc captures an initial quickstart script, fallback routing rules, minimal telemetry contract, and short experiment plan.

Goals (MVP):
1) 5-minute developer onboarding path: npm install, export ...

text:
Purpose:
A compact, actionable log of the last fortnight's onboarding eval regressions and the leading hypotheses we want to test. This is deliberately a working doc — quick notes, examples, and narrow experiments we can run in the next 2 weeks.

Executive summary (TL;DR):
- Overall pass rate on new onboarding prompt sets dropped ~4.8 percentage points vs baseline (7-day rolling).
- Largest degradation is in 'intent-preservation' tests for short chat prompts (avg. F1 down 9%).
- Three dominant failure clusters: hallucinations about proprietary product names, truncation-related context loss for >512-token histories, and temperature-sensitive creativity mismatches.
- Immediate actions: 1) pin a canary model variant for the most-critical customers, 2) run targeted KV/cache sanity checks for long-turn conversations, 3) start 2 small experiments (context-window padding vs. explicit system ...

text:
Purpose:
This is a compact, action-oriented launch readiness and KPI playbook with a bias for stability-first decisions. The "Stability Sherpa" concept: guide the launch through terrain (ramp phases) with conservative checkpoints, clear signal thresholds, and pre-approved fallback moves so engineering and product can move quickly without guessing.

Scope:
- Applies to any product-ops led feature activation that routes traffic to Redwood hosted inference (hosted API) or Dedicated pools.
- Focus: preflight signals, KPI targets per phase, quick triage/contingency actions.
- Not covered: long-term post-mortem templates (link to PMR).

Quick summary (one-line):
If 3 consecutive signal probes breach thresholds in a phase, pause ramp and run the phase rollback checklist.

Launch phases and goals:
1) Canary (0.1% traffic, 1–2 hours)
 - Goal: functional correctness, no infra error spikes, late...

preview:
Purpose:
This is a working promptset + playbook for onboarding new generation/refactor-capable models into the canned code-refactor regression harness. The focus is small-to-medium code rewrite tasks that must preserve behavior (compile + unit tests) and meet stylistic constraints (linting, idiomatic patterns).

Sco...
text:
Purpose:
This is a working promptset + playbook for onboarding new generation/refactor-capable models into the canned code-refactor regression harness. The focus is small-to-medium code rewrite tasks that must preserve behavior (compile + unit tests) and meet stylistic constraints (linting, idiomatic patterns).

Scope and goals:
- Evaluate model ability to perform targeted refactors (extract method, rename symbol, simplify control flow, reduce cyclomatic complexity) for Python, JavaScript, and Java. C++ smoke cases included for compilation sensitivity.
- Gate models using three concrete signals: compile success rate (per-language), unit-test pass rate (preserve behavior), and style regressions (lint/diff score).
- Create short, repeatable prompts that can be used in canary/onboarding runs and regression checks.

Why this is different from existing prompt suites:
- Emphasizes increment...

text:
Purpose:
This is a lightweight, experiment-driven 30/60/90 onboarding plan designed to surface early product impact and technical blockers for customers adopting Redwood Inference. The emphasis is on short, measurable experiments that validate integration assumptions (latency, cost per token, SDK fit) and produce at least one customer-visible win within 30 days.

Audience:
- Customer Success Manager (CSM) owning the engagement
- Customer engineering lead / ML engineer
- Solutions engineer assigned to the account

Outcome hypothesis (example):
Within 90 days the customer has:
- Integrated Redwood API for a single, high-value feature (chat or summarization) in staging,
- Measured end-to-end median P95 latency and unit cost under target thresholds,
- A documented rollout plan for production and a renewal signal tied to usage and SLA requirements.

Success metrics (agree these with custom...
